zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of active causal structure learning, where an agent aims to identify the causal graph of a system by performing interventions. While existing approaches often rely on greedy heuristics or assume a specific functional form, we propose a principled Bayesian framework for active causal structure learning. Our approach maintains a posterior distribution over causal graphs and selects interventions that maximize the expected information gain. To make this computationally tractable, we introduce a variational approximation that allows for efficient inference and decision-making. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on synthetic and real-world causal discovery tasks, showing that it outperforms existing active learning strategies.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 35 (NeurIPS 2022)
